step.title: Dispatch Reviewer Subagent (Conditional)
step.goal: For high-stakes primary tags (feature, exploration, infrastructure), dispatch the ticket-reviewer subagent for fresh-context review. Merge its findings with the self-review report.

substep.title: Decide Whether to Dispatch
substep.action:
Per spec §10.1: - feature / exploration / infrastructure → DISPATCH - bug / hotfix / chore → SKIP (self-review suffices)
Store as {{dispatch_reviewer}}: true | false.

check.action:
Invoke ticket-reviewer via the Task tool with a prompt containing: - Ticket file path - Research file path - Questions file path - Primary tag - Optional context: any specific concerns the agent wants the reviewer to focus on (e.g., "the AC section came in shorter than estimated; check for completeness")
The subagent operates in fresh context — it does NOT see the self-review findings. This is deliberate; the subagent's value is a second-opinion diagnostic from an audience that hasn't lived through the Q↔R loop.
Wait for the subagent to return its finding report.
Skip. Note in session: "Reviewer subagent skipped (primary tag is {{primary_tag}}; self-review only per spec §10.1)."

check.if: dispatch_reviewer is true and subagent returned findings
check.action:
Merge the subagent's findings into the self-review report. Process:
1. Parse the subagent's finding report (which uses the same severity-ladder output format) 2. For each subagent finding, check if a self-review finding flagged the same issue (compare location and topic). If yes, deduplicate (keep one; note "flagged by both" in the merged entry). If no, add as a new finding. 3. Re-tally counts and re-derive the verdict per the ladder.
Store as {{merged_findings_report}}: the final consolidated report ready for step 4.

substep.title: Display Merged Report

section.title: The ticket-reviewer subagent is read-only
section.description: Per Lock 29 + spec §10.1, ticket-reviewer's edit permission is denied. It reports; ticketsmith (this skill) applies fixes. Do NOT expect the subagent to edit the ticket. Do NOT try to delegate fix application to it.

section.title: Dedup discipline
section.description: Two reviews finding the same defect is a STRONG signal — keep the entry, note "flagged by both reviews", and treat with the higher of the two severities. Avoid presenting the user with duplicate findings; they'll lose patience and dismiss the whole report.

section.title: Why no subagent for bug/hotfix/chore
section.description: Per spec: these tags carry lower stakes; self-review provides sufficient quality assurance. Dispatching a subagent for a 2-line chore ticket is overhead. Stick to the spec; if a user explicitly asks for fresh-context review on a chore, accommodate — but don't default to it.
